name: Public Infrastructure & Utilities Subpack
description: Vertical-specialized value pack for public infrastructure and utilities: water/wastewater, transit, roads/bridges, airports/ports, energy authorities, broadband, waste management, smart city, emergency communications, and grid modernization. Adds vertical-specific pains, KPIs, formulas, benchmarks, personas, signals, discovery questions, objections, technology systems, regulatory factors, and buying triggers.

pains.name: Water Main Break Rate Exceeding Tolerance
pains.description: Water distribution systems experience main break rates >25 per 100 miles/year, indicating pipe age, material failure, pressure management failure, and deferred replacement. Each break costs $5K–$50K in direct repair plus indirect water loss, traffic disruption, and potential contamination risk.
symptoms: Emergency repair overtime >20% of maintenance budget
sources: EPA Drinking Water Needs Survey 7th Assessment

pains.name: Transit On-Time Performance Below Threshold
pains.description: Transit on-time performance <75% signals schedule reliability breakdown, eroding ridership recovery and increasing operating costs through overtime, deadhead, and customer service complaints. Poor OTP correlates with fare revenue loss and service cuts.
symptoms: Customer complaints about lateness up >20% YoY

pains.name: SCADA/OT Cybersecurity Exposure
pains.description: Infrastructure operational technology (SCADA, DMS, AMI) faces increasing nation-state and ransomware targeting. Many systems lack network segmentation, have default passwords, run on unsupported OS, and have no OT-specific SOC. NERC CIP applies only to bulk electric; water/transit OT often unregulated.
symptoms: Default or shared passwords on PLCs

pains.name: Bridge Condition Rating Deterioration
pains.description: State and local bridge inventories show >7% structurally deficient or poor condition. SD bridges create load restrictions, detour costs, emergency closure risk, and liability exposure. Federal NBIS requires biennial inspection but funding for rehabilitation lags.

pains.name: Airport Operational Efficiency & Part 139 Compliance
pains.description: Airports face Part 139 inspection pressures, ARFF staffing requirements, runway condition reporting mandates, and FMO/fueling safety rules. Manual inspection processes, paper logbooks, and siloed systems create compliance risk and operational inefficiency.
symptoms: ARFF response time >3 minutes to midpoint

pains.name: Broadband Deployment Cost Overrun & BEAD Compliance
pains.description: BEAD and other federal broadband programs require complex compliance: subgrantee selection, preference scoring, high-cost threshold compliance, local coordination, and deployment reporting. Underestimation of make-ready, permitting delays, and supply chain issues drive cost overruns 20-40%.
symptoms: Low ACP adoption in served areas

pains.name: Wastewater Collection System I/I and SSOs
pains.description: Infiltration and inflow (I/I) overload collection systems, causing sanitary sewer overflows (SSOs) during wet weather. SSOs violate CWA permits, trigger EPA enforcement, and expose agencies to consent decrees. I/I reduction requires targeted rehabilitation but sewer assessment backlogs delay action.
symptoms: I/I volume >40% of dry weather flow
Consent decree or EPA enforcement present

pains.name: Fleet Electrification & Alternative Fuel Transition
pains.description: Federal and state mandates (EO 14057, state ZEV mandates) require fleet electrification. Infrastructure agencies face depot power capacity limits, EV procurement delays, charging infrastructure gaps, and workforce training shortfalls. Total cost of ownership uncertainty blocks capital decisions.
symptoms: Depot electrical capacity insufficient for plan

pains.name: Energy Distribution Grid Resilience & Outage Duration
pains.description: Public power and IOU territories face increasing SAIDI/SAIFI from aging distribution infrastructure, storm intensity, and vegetation management gaps. Municipal utilities with overhead systems see SAIDI >4 hours/year. Underground systems face longer restoration times for cable faults.

pains.name: Smart City IoT Integration & Data Silos
pains.description: Smart city deployments (sensors, cameras, digital twins) generate data that sits in vendor-specific platforms without integration. Traffic, parking, air quality, and utility data don't combine for holistic urban management. Interoperability standards (OCF, oneM2M) remain immature.
symptoms: IoT platforms >5 with no central data layer
Digital twin incomplete or stale >30 days
No API between traffic and signal systems

pains.name: Emergency Communications (911/PSAP) NextGen Transition
pains.description: PSAPs face NextGen 911 transition requiring IP-based call handling, GIS integration, text-to-911, and multimedia support. Legacy CAMA trunking, ALI database dependencies, and vendor lock-in create transition risk. Staffing shortages compound technology complexity.
symptoms: NextGen vendor contract >5 years old with no upgrade path

pains.name: Waste & Recycling Contamination and Market Volatility
pains.description: Recycling contamination rates >25% increase processing costs and reduce commodity revenue. China's National Sword and shifting export markets have destabilized recycling economics. Municipalities face service cuts, higher tipping fees, and public backlash.
symptoms: Processing cost per ton >landfill tip fee

pains.name: Port Terminal Throughput & Drayage Inefficiency
pains.description: Port authorities face container dwell time growth, chassis shortages, drayage truck turnaround delays, and yard congestion. Demurrage/detention disputes and trucker access issues create operational and regulatory friction. Emissions regulations add compliance cost.

pains.name: Rate Affordability and Shutoff Crisis
pains.description: Water and energy rate increases to fund infrastructure investment create affordability crises. Shutoff rates rise, triggering public health concerns, regulatory scrutiny, and political backlash. Low-income assistance programs (LIHEAP, H2O Help) are under-enrolled and underfunded.
symptoms: Media coverage of shutoff-related health incidents

pains.name: Transit Fare Evasion and Revenue Leakage
pains.description: Honors-system and proof-of-payment transit operations face fare evasion rates 5-15%, eroding already-weak farebox recovery. Enforcement costs, citation processing, and public perception challenges complicate revenue protection strategies.

pains.name: PFAS and Emerging Contaminant Compliance
pains.description: EPA PFAS MCLs and emerging contaminant monitoring create massive analytical, treatment, and communication challenges for water systems. Small systems (<10,000 population) face disproportionate cost burden for GAC/IX/RO treatment upgrades.

pains.name: Airfield Pavement Condition Index Decline
pains.description: Airport runways, taxiways, and aprons deteriorate under aircraft loads and weather, requiring PCI assessment and maintenance. Deferred pavement maintenance increases Foreign Object Debris (FOD) risk, aircraft damage exposure, and full reconstruction cost (5-10x maintenance cost multiplier).
symptoms: Pavement maintenance budget <2% of replacement value

pains.name: Distributed Energy Resource (DER) Interconnection Backlog
pains.description: Rooftop solar, battery storage, and EV charger interconnection requests overwhelm utility review capacity. Queue delays >12 months create customer dissatisfaction, regulatory complaints, and lost clean energy targets. Hosting capacity analysis often missing or outdated.

kpis.name: Water Main Break Rate
kpis.formula: COUNT(Main Breaks in Period) / (Total Distribution Miles / 100)

kpis.name: Unaccounted-for Water (UFW)
kpis.formula: (Water Produced - Water Billed + Authorized Unbilled) / Water Produced * 100%

kpis.name: Pipe Replacement Rate
kpis.formula: Miles Replaced or Rehabilitated / Total Distribution Miles * 100%

kpis.name: Transit On-Time Performance
kpis.formula: On-Time Trips / Total Scheduled Trips * 100%

kpis.name: Operating Cost Per Vehicle Revenue Mile
kpis.formula: Total Operating Cost / Vehicle Revenue Miles
kpis.benchmarkRange: Bus: $10-15; rail: $15-25; best-in-class: 10% below peer median

kpis.name: Farebox Recovery Ratio
kpis.formula: Fare Revenue / Total Operating Cost * 100%

kpis.name: OT Network Security Posture Score
kpis.formula: AVG(Segmentation Score + Access Control Score + Monitoring Score + Patch Score + Backup Score) on 0-100 scale
kpis.benchmarkRange: NERC CIP compliant: >80; best practice: >85; critical: <50

kpis.name: SCADA System Availability
kpis.formula: (Total Scheduled Uptime - Unplanned Downtime) / Total Scheduled Uptime * 100%

kpis.name: Mean Time to Restore OT Service
kpis.formula: AVG(Restoration Time - Outage Start Time) for OT system outages

kpis.name: Structurally Deficient Bridge Percentage
kpis.formula: Structurally Deficient Bridges / Total Inventory * 100%
kpis.benchmarkRange: Target: <5%; DOT goal: near zero; concerning: >8%

kpis.name: Bridge Condition Index (BCI)
kpis.formula: AVG(Element Condition Rating weighted by element quantity) across all bridges

kpis.name: Pavement Condition Index (PCI) - Roads
kpis.formula: AVG(Pavement Condition Rating) weighted by lane miles

kpis.name: ARFF Response Time to Midpoint
kpis.formula: AVG(Response Time to Runway Midpoint for ARFF Vehicles)
kpis.benchmarkRange: FAA Part 139: <3 minutes (180s) to midpoint; best: <120s

kpis.name: Airfield Pavement Condition Index
kpis.formula: AVG(PCS/PCI) weighted by pavement area (runway, taxiway, apron)

kpis.name: FOD Detection Event Rate
kpis.formula: FOD Incidents per 100,000 Aircraft Operations

kpis.name: Broadband Cost Per Location Passed
kpis.formula: Total Deployment Cost / Locations Passed or Connected
kpis.benchmarkRange: Dense urban: <$2,500; rural: $4,000-$8,000; BEAD threshold varies

kpis.name: BEAD Milestone Compliance Rate
kpis.formula: Milestones Met on Schedule / Total Milestones * 100%

kpis.name: Broadband Adoption Rate (Served Areas)
kpis.formula: Subscribers / Serviceable Locations * 100%

kpis.name: Sanitary Sewer Overflow (SSO) Rate
kpis.formula: SSO Events / 100 Miles of Sewer * 100%

kpis.name: I/I Volume Ratio
kpis.formula: Wet Weather Flow Increase / Dry Weather Flow * 100%

kpis.name: Collection System Rehabilitation Rate
kpis.formula: Miles Rehabilitated (CIPP, slip-lining, etc.) / Total Collection Miles * 100%

kpis.name: Fleet Electrification Percentage
kpis.formula: Electric / Alternative Fuel Vehicles / Total Fleet * 100%

kpis.name: EV Charging Utilization Rate
kpis.formula: Charging Sessions / (Charger Count * Operating Days) * 100%

kpis.name: Fleet Vehicle Availability
kpis.formula: Available Vehicles / Total Fleet Vehicles * 100%

kpis.name: System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI)
kpis.formula: SUM(Customer Interruption Duration) / Total Customers
kpis.benchmarkRange: Best practice: <100 min; median US: ~200 min; concerning: >300 min

formulas.name: Water Main Break Cost Avoidance via Pipe Replacement
formulas.formulaExpression: V = (Baseline_Break_Rate - Target_Break_Rate) * System_Miles / 100 * Avg_Cost_Per_Break + Water_Loss_Value + Emergency_Overtime_Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if break history and cost data available; MEDIUM if using AWWA estimates; LOW if no pipe inventory
formulas.exampleCalculation: Baseline 30 breaks/100mi→15, 500 miles, $15K/break, $200K water loss, $100K overtime: (15*5*$15K)+$200K+$100K = $1.425M annual

formulas.name: Transit On-Time Performance Revenue Recovery
formulas.formulaExpression: V = (Target_OTP - Baseline_OTP) * Ridership_Elasticity * Annual_Ridership * Avg_Fare + Overtime_Avoidance + Customer_Service_Cost_Reduction
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM (ridership elasticity varies by market; validated with NTD data preferred); LOW if no ridership model
formulas.exampleCalculation: 72%→82% OTP, elasticity 0.5, 10M ridership, $2 fare, $300K overtime, $100K CS: 10%*0.5*10M*$2+$400K = $1.4M+$400K = $1.8M

formulas.name: OT Cybersecurity Risk Reduction Value
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM (OT incident cost highly variable; Dragos/CISA data helpful); LOW if no OT asset inventory
formulas.exampleCalculation: 5%→1% probability, $10M incident cost, $500K compliance, $200K insurance: 4%*$10M+$700K = $4.7M annual

formulas.name: Bridge Load Posting Elimination Value
formulas.formulaExpression: V = SUM(Load_Posted_Bridges * Avg_Detour_Cost_Per_Bridge * Traffic_Volume) + Emergency_Repair_Avoidance + SD_Elimination_Grant_Value
requiredInputs: Federal grant value for SD elimination
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if NBI and traffic data available; MEDIUM if using FHWA average detour costs; LOW if no traffic count
formulas.exampleCalculation: 20 load-posted bridges, $50K detour cost each, 500 vehicles/day: 20*$50K + $2M emergency = $3M annual

formulas.name: Airport Part 139 Compliance Automation Value
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM (AIP gain speculative; FTE savings calculable); LOW if no inspection time study

formulas.name: Broadband Deployment Cost Efficiency Gain
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM (BEAD program new; limited historical data); LOW if no location data

formulas.name: SSO/I-I Reduction Value
formulas.formulaExpression: V = SSO_Events_Eliminated * Avg_SSO_Cost + Wet_Weather_Treatment_Cost_Avoidance + Consent_Decree_Compliance_Value + CWA_Penalty_Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if SSO log and cost data available; MEDIUM if using EPA estimates; LOW if no flow monitoring
formulas.exampleCalculation: 15→5 SSOs, $100K avg, $1M treatment, $2M consent decree, $500K penalty: 10*$100K+$3.5M = $4.5M

formulas.name: Fleet Electrification TCO Savings
formulas.formulaExpression: V = (ICE_OandM_Cost - EV_OandM_Cost) * EVs_Deployed + Fuel_Cost_Savings + Carbon_Credit_Value + Grant_Offset - Infrastructure_Upfront_Cost_Amortized
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM (EV costs evolving; electricity rates volatile); LOW if no pilot data

formulas.name: Distribution Reliability Improvement Value
formulas.formulaExpression: V = (Baseline_SAIDI - Target_SAIDI) / 60 * Customer_Count * VOLL + Storm_Hardening_Avoided_Cost + Vegetation_Optimization_Savings
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if outage cost data available; MEDIUM if using DOE VOLL estimates ($5-$15/kWh); LOW if no customer count

formulas.name: Smart City IoT Net Operational Value
formulas.formulaExpression: V = Traffic_Efficiency_Value + Energy_Savings + Safety_Improvement_Value + Predictive_Maintenance_Value - Sensor_Network_TCO - Integration_Cost
formulas.confidenceRules: LOW (emerging, limited long-term data; city-specific); MEDIUM if pilot data available
formulas.exampleCalculation: $800K traffic + $400K energy + $300K safety + $600K maintenance - $1.2M TCO - $400K integration = $500K net

formulas.name: NextGen 911 Transition Efficiency Value
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM (NG911 costs vary by state architecture); LOW if no PSAP cost baseline
formulas.exampleCalculation: $300K trunk + $500K dispatch + $200K productivity + $100K text - $800K amortized = $300K net

formulas.name: Recycling Contamination Reduction Value
formulas.formulaExpression: V = Contamination_Reduction_% * Tons_Processed * (Contamination_Premium_Cost - Sorting_Cost) + Commodity_Revenue_Recovery + Landfill_Diversion_Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if MRF cost data available; MEDIUM if using SWANA estimates; LOW if no tonnage data
formulas.exampleCalculation: 25%→15% on 50K tons, $40/ton premium, $30 sorting, $200K commodity, $300K landfill: 10%*50K*$10+$500K = $50K+$500K = $550K

benchmarks.name: Water Main Break Rate (National Median)
benchmarks.source: AWWA / EPA Drinking Water Needs Survey

benchmarks.name: BEAD Cost Per Location (National Average Estimate)

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Break rate >25/100 miles/year; repeat breaks on same line >2 per year; age >50 years for cast iron
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Pipe inventory at failure threshold; reactive maintenance dominating; replacement backlog growing; risk of large-diameter catastrophic failure

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: OTP <75% for 2+ quarters; operator vacancy >15%; farebox recovery <20%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Service-quality spiral risk; ridership loss accelerating; revenue model broken; potential service cuts and political backlash

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Flat OT network; default PLC passwords; remote access unmanaged; no OT IDS; SCADA on Windows 7/XP
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Critical infrastructure exposed to ransomware and nation-state attack; potential for service disruption and safety incident; regulatory enforcement likely

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: SD percentage >7%; load postings increasing; inspection backlog >12 months
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Federal funding insufficient for state of good repair; emergency closure risk; freight diversion costs; potential federal enforcement

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Part 139 findings >2 per inspection; ARFF response >180s; PCI <70 on primary runway
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: FAA enforcement risk; potential certificate action; airline operational concerns; capital needs urgent but AIP competitive

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: BEAD subgrantee selection delayed >6 months; permitting >50% of build schedule; make-ready >30% of aerial budget
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: NTIA compliance deadline at risk; federal funding lapse possible; deployment cost unsustainable; digital equity gap persists

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: SSO events >10/year; I/I >50% of dry flow; consent decree or EPA enforcement present
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Collection system at capacity; wet weather bypasses threatening CWA permit; capital program urgently needed but rate capacity limited

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: EV procurement delayed >12 months; depot electrical capacity insufficient; maintenance bay lacking EV capability
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Federal/state ZEV mandates at risk; total cost of ownership analysis incomplete; infrastructure upgrade needed before fleet procurement

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: SAIDI >240 min; SAIFI >2.0; major event days >5/year; vegetation outages >30%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Distribution infrastructure aging; storm intensity exceeding design; vegetation program underfunded; customer satisfaction and regulatory risk rising

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: IoT platforms >5 with no central data layer; digital twin stale >30 days; sensor uptime <90%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Vendor silos preventing city-wide optimization; digital twin not decision-ready; sensor network ROI unproven; data governance immature

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Text-to-911 not deployed; CAMA still primary; GIS accuracy <95%; PSAP turnover >15%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Legacy 911 at end of life; NG911 vendor lock-in risk; staffing crisis compounding technology transition; FCC and state deadline pressure

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Contamination >25%; commodity revenue down >30%; processing cost >landfill tip fee
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Recycling program financially unsustainable; public support eroding; MRF viability at risk; potential service cutback or program termination

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Truck turn >75 min; dwell >6 days; chassis availability <85%; demurrage disputes increasing
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Drayage capacity constrained; yard congestion creating vessel delay; shipper dissatisfaction; regulatory attention on demurrage; emissions exceedance risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Shutoff rate >5%; rate increase >5% for 3 years; delinquency >8%; low-income enrollment <20%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Rate trajectory unsustainable for low-income households; public health and equity risk; political pressure for rate freeze; revenue collection risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Evasion >8% by observation; revenue discrepancy >2% vs model; citation collection <30%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Fare collection system ineffective; revenue recovery potential significant; enforcement cost may exceed recovery at current efficiency; AFC upgrade needed

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: PFAS detection >proposed MCL; treatment pilot not started; lab wait >30 days; CWSRF pending >12 months
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: MCL effective date approaching; small systems face disproportionate cost; public communication challenge; potential EPA enforcement if untreated

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: PCI <70 on primary runway; FOD incidents >2/year; crack sealing backlog >12 months
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Pavement lifecycle compressed; reconstruction cost 5-10x maintenance; potential weight restriction or closure; AIP funding competitive

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Queue >1,000 pending; review time >180 days; rejection/modification >40%; no hosting capacity map
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: DER growth exceeding utility planning capacity; regulatory complaints rising; clean energy targets at risk; distribution system reinforcement needed

personas.name: Water Utility Director / General Manager
personas.role: Leads drinking water and/or wastewater utility; owns treatment, distribution, regulatory compliance, and capital planning
personas.goals: SDWA compliance; zero violations; rate stability; infrastructure replacement; CCR transparency; lead service line elimination; PFAS compliance
personas.pressures: EPA enforcement; consent decrees; rate case hearings; aging workforce; emerging contaminants; climate resilience; CWSRF/DRF funding competition
personas.trustedEvidence: AWWA standards; EPA guidance; SDWIS data; ASCE report card; rate study consultants; peer utility benchmarking (Alliance for Water Efficiency)
personas.dislikedClaims: Technology replacing operators; 'smart water' without cybersecurity plan; rate increases without demonstrated need; vendor lock-in in SCADA

personas.name: Transit Authority General Manager / CEO
personas.role: Leads public transit agency; owns service planning, operations, maintenance, labor relations, and ridership recovery
personas.goals: Ridership recovery; on-time performance; fare revenue; operating cost control; operator recruitment; fleet modernization; equity in service
personas.pressures: FTA compliance; NTD reporting; operator shortage; post-pandemic revenue shortfall; ADA compliance; zero-emission mandates; labor negotiations
personas.trustedEvidence: NTD data; APTA reports; FTA guidance; peer agency benchmarking; ridership studies; union agreements; passenger surveys
personas.dislikedClaims: Fare technology that ignores equity; 'automation' threatening operator jobs; unrealistic ridership projections; vendor exclusivity in AFC

personas.name: Public Works Director / County Engineer
personas.role: Leads roads, bridges, fleet, facilities, and often solid waste; owns infrastructure condition, maintenance, and construction programs
personas.goals: Road/bridge condition improvement; snow removal reliability; fleet availability; cost per lane mile; safety incident reduction; ADA compliance
personas.pressures: NBI ratings; FHWA STIP requirements; fuel price volatility; salt/material cost spikes; workforce aging; winter weather intensity; equipment replacement
personas.trustedEvidence: FHWA guidance; APWA benchmarking; state DOT standards; NBI data; pavement management system data; crash statistics; AASHTO standards
personas.dislikedClaims: Unproven materials; technology without field validation; 'smart' infrastructure without maintenance plan; consultant-driven overdesign

personas.name: Grid Modernization Engineer / Utility Engineering Director
personas.role: Owns distribution system planning, DER integration, ADMS/DMS, SCADA, and grid hardening; leads capital programs for substations and feeders
personas.goals: SAIDI/SAIFI reduction; DER interconnection speed; ADMS deployment; storm hardening; outage management; voltage optimization; EV readiness
personas.pressures: NERC CIP (if applicable); state RPS mandates; FERC Order 2023; interconnection queue backlog; supply chain for transformers; vegetation management; climate resilience
personas.trustedEvidence: IEEE standards; NREL research; EIA data; DOE Grid Modernization Lab Consortium; state PUC orders; Utility Analytics Institute benchmarking
personas.dislikedClaims: Proprietary protocols preventing interoperability; 'AI grid' without data foundation; unrealistic DER penetration assumptions; vendor lock-in in ADMS

personas.name: Airport Operations Manager / Director of Operations
personas.role: Owns airfield operations, ARFF, snow removal, terminal operations, and Part 139 compliance; manages 24/7 operational tempo
personas.goals: Part 139 zero findings; ARFF readiness; FOD elimination; on-time gate performance; snow plan execution; terminal safety; emergency preparedness
personas.pressures: FAA inspections; airline SLA penalties; TSA security directives; weather events; ARFF staffing; equipment availability; AIP grant compliance; noise complaints
personas.trustedEvidence: FAA ACs; ACRP reports; NTSB recommendations; airport council (ACI-NA) data; airline scorecards; snow plan after-action reports; incident data
personas.dislikedClaims: Technology that distracts from operational awareness; 'automated' systems without human override; unproven airfield tech; compliance shortcuts

personas.name: Broadband Program Director / Digital Equity Officer
personas.role: Leads broadband deployment, digital equity, BEAD/NTIA compliance, and subscriber adoption; manages subgrantees and local coordination
personas.goals: 100% broadband coverage; BEAD milestone compliance; digital navigator deployment; low-income adoption; affordability; digital literacy; vendor diversity
personas.pressures: NTIA/BEAD timeline; FCC challenge process; make-ready delays; permitting variance across jurisdictions; supply chain; workforce shortage; ACP sunset; digital equity grant compliance
personas.trustedEvidence: FCC BDC data; NTIA guidance; Pew Research; state broadband office data; Benton Institute; subgrantee performance reports; adoption surveys
personas.dislikedClaims: Technology-first without community engagement; overbuild without adoption plan; 'one vendor' deployment; unrealistic cost per passing estimates

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current water main break rate per 100 miles, and how has it trended over the past five years?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Assess pipe infrastructure condition and deferred maintenance backlog
followUpQuestions: What percentage of your distribution system is cast iron or asbestos cement?
Do you have a risk-based pipe replacement plan?
What is your annual pipe replacement rate as a percentage of total system?

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you currently track and report on-time performance, and what is your target versus actual for the last 12 months?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Measure transit service reliability and identify root causes of delay
followUpQuestions: What percentage of delays are attributed to traffic versus mechanical versus operator availability?
Do you use real-time dispatching or schedule adherence tools?
How has OTP correlated with ridership trends?

discoveryQuestions.question: Describe your current OT/ICS cybersecurity posture. Is your SCADA network segmented from IT?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Identify critical infrastructure cyber exposure and compliance gaps
followUpQuestions: Have you conducted an OT-specific vulnerability assessment?
Do you have an OT incident response plan?
Are remote access connections to SCADA managed through a jump server or VPN?

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of your bridge inventory is structurally deficient or in poor condition, and what is your annual rehabilitation spend?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Quantify bridge backlog and funding adequacy for state of good repair
followUpQuestions: How many bridges currently have load postings?
What is your bridge condition index trend?
How competitive is your STIP/TIP for bridge projects?

discoveryQuestions.question: What was the outcome of your most recent FAA Part 139 inspection, and how do you manage ARFF readiness and snow removal planning?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Evaluate airport compliance posture and operational readiness risk
followUpQuestions: How do you track ARFF response time to the runway midpoint?
Is your snow plan integrated with operational decision-making?
What Part 139 findings are currently open?

discoveryQuestions.question: Where are you in the BEAD subgrantee selection process, and what are your biggest deployment blockers?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Assess broadband program maturity and compliance risk
followUpQuestions: What percentage of your deployment is aerial vs underground?
How do you manage permitting across jurisdictions?
What is your current cost estimate per location passed?

discoveryQuestions.question: How many SSO events did you report last year, and what is your current I/I volume as a percentage of dry weather flow?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Quantify collection system capacity and wet weather risk
followUpQuestions: Do you have a consent decree or EPA enforcement action?
What percentage of your collection system has been assessed for I/I?
What is your rehabilitation rate per year?

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your fleet electrification target, and have you assessed depot electrical capacity for the full plan?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Determine ZEV readiness and infrastructure upgrade gap
followUpQuestions: What percentage of your fleet is currently zero-emission?
Have you completed a TCO analysis comparing ICE to EV?
What is the status of your maintenance bay upgrades?

discoveryQuestions.question: What are your SAIDI and SAIFI figures for the last reporting year, and how much is attributable to major events versus routine?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Measure distribution reliability and storm resilience investment need
followUpQuestions: What percentage of outages are vegetation-related?
Do you have a targeted feeder hardening program?
What is your underground versus overhead ratio?

discoveryQuestions.question: How many IoT/sensor platforms are deployed across your city, and is the data integrated into a central analytics layer?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Assess smart city maturity and data silo barriers
followUpQuestions: Do you have a published data governance policy?
What is your sensor uptime across the network?
Is there a digital twin in operation?

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current 911 call answer time at the 90th percentile, and have you deployed text-to-911 and NG911?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Evaluate emergency communications readiness and modernization status
followUpQuestions: What is your PSAP staffing turnover rate?
Is your GIS data accurate enough for Z-axis location?
What is your NG911 vendor contract status?

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current recycling contamination rate, and how has commodity revenue trended over the past three years?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Assess recycling program financial sustainability and operational efficiency
followUpQuestions: What is your processing cost per ton at the MRF?
Have you considered pay-as-you-throw or deposit models?
What is your diversion rate trend?

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your average truck turn time, and how do you manage container dwell and chassis availability?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Quantify port throughput efficiency and drayage bottleneck
followUpQuestions: What percentage of turn time is queue versus transaction?
Do you have a truck appointment system?
How do you manage demurrage/dispute volume?

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of your accounts are shut off annually for non-payment, and how do you manage affordability programs?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Assess customer affordability crisis and revenue collection risk
followUpQuestions: What is your annual rate increase trajectory?
What percentage of eligible households are enrolled in assistance?
Have you conducted an EPA-style affordability assessment?

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your observed fare evasion rate, and what percentage of issued citations are actually collected?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Quantify revenue leakage and enforcement effectiveness
followUpQuestions: Do you operate proof-of-payment or gated fare collection?
What is your revenue recovery ratio?
How has AFC upgrade planning progressed?

discoveryQuestions.question: Have you detected PFAS in your source water or distribution, and what is your treatment upgrade timeline?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Identify emerging contaminant compliance risk and capital need
followUpQuestions: What is your laboratory capacity and turnaround time?
Have you initiated pilot testing for GAC/IX/RO?
What is your CWSRF/DWSRF application status?

discoveryQuestions.question: What is the condition of your primary runway by PCI, and when was the last pavement management survey?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Assess airfield infrastructure condition and capital timing
followUpQuestions: How many FOD incidents were recorded last year?
What is your pavement maintenance budget as % of replacement value?
Are there any weight restrictions contemplated?

discoveryQuestions.question: How many DER interconnection requests are pending, and what is your average review time from application to approval?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Quantify DER integration bottleneck and grid readiness
followUpQuestions: Do you publish hosting capacity maps?
What percentage of applications require modification or rejection?
How does interconnection queue management compare to your state RPS timeline?

objections.objection: We already have a capital improvement plan that addresses this. We don't need another solution.
objections.rootCauseAnalysis: Often the CIP is underfunded (funded at <30% of need), or the pain is operational (not capital). The CIP may not include technology, data, or workforce components needed for execution.
objections.counterApproach: Validate CIP funding ratio vs. EPA/AWWA documented need. Identify whether the pain has an operational/digital dimension not in the CIP. Frame as CIP acceleration and execution support, not replacement.

objections.objection: Our rates are already too high; we can't afford new technology or consulting.
objections.rootCauseAnalysis: Rate pressure is real but often driven by reactive spending (emergency repairs, overtime, penalties) that exceeds the cost of proactive technology. The affordability argument confuses capex with lifecycle cost.
objections.counterApproach: Quantify reactive cost as percentage of operating budget. Show how technology reduces emergency spend and deferrable rate increases. Reference EPA affordability framework (combined water+wastewater <4.5% MHI).

objections.objection: We are a small system / agency; this solution is built for large utilities.
objections.rootCauseAnalysis: Small systems (<10,000) face disproportionate regulatory burden but lack staff for complex implementations. Vendor solutions often ignore this segment.
objections.counterApproach: Reference shared-services, cooperative purchasing, and cloud-based delivery models that reduce per-system cost. Cite EPA small system compliance assistance and rural utility service programs.

objections.objection: We have grants already covering this; we don't need to spend our own money.
objections.rootCauseAnalysis: Federal grants (BIL, IIJA, IRA) are competitive and time-limited. Match requirements, Buy America, prevailing wage, and local share requirements still require agency investment. Grant-funded projects often lack O&M planning.
objections.counterApproach: Map grant lifecycle: application → award → obligation → match → O&M. Identify gaps where technology, planning, or compliance support is not grant-eligible but required for success.

objections.objection: Our SCADA/OT systems are air-gapped; cybersecurity is not a concern.
objections.rootCauseAnalysis: True air-gapping is rare; remote access for vendors, VPNs, and USB maintenance breaks the gap. Nation-state actors (Russia, Iran) have specifically targeted water and energy OT. CISA and EPA guidance contradict this claim.
objections.counterApproach: Request network diagram walkthrough. Reference CISA Alert AA22-103A (Iranian cyber targeting of water) and EPA guidance. Offer OT-safe assessment that does not disrupt operations.

objections.objection: Our transit riders won't accept fare enforcement or new technology.
objections.rootCauseAnalysis: Equity concerns are legitimate but addressable. Proof-of-payment systems in peer cities have succeeded with equity-centered design (citation diversion programs, reduced fares). The objection conflates enforcement with collection technology.
objections.counterApproach: Reference TransitCenter and UCLA equity studies. Separate AFC technology (tap-to-pay, mobile) from enforcement. Demonstrate how modern AFC improves equity (reduced cash handling, auto-enrollment in discount programs).

objections.objection: We tried smart city / digital twin before and it failed.
objections.rootCauseAnalysis: Smart city failures typically stem from vendor lock-in, lack of data governance, absence of operational ownership, and unrealistic scope. Technology was not the failure—implementation planning was.
objections.counterApproach: Acknowledge failure mode. Propose phased approach with clear operational owner, open standards, and measurable pilot KPIs. Reference NIST smart city framework and ICMA maturity model.

objections.objection: Our labor union will block any automation or operational change.
objections.rootCauseAnalysis: Union resistance is strongest when change is presented as job elimination rather than job transformation. Infrastructure unions (AFSCME, ATU, IBEW) have supported technology that improves safety and skill development.
objections.counterApproach: Frame as upskilling, safety improvement, and workload reduction (not headcount reduction). Reference labor-management partnership examples. Involve union early in design.

objections.objection: We need to wait for our next rate case / budget cycle / bond issuance.
objections.rootCauseAnalysis: Budget cycles create artificial delay. However, federal grants, ARPA/SLFRF, and emergency procurement authorities can bypass normal cycles. The objection may indicate risk aversion rather than true fiscal constraint.
objections.counterApproach: Map alternative funding sources (grants, low-interest loans, performance contracting, cooperative purchasing). Demonstrate that delay increases risk and cost. Pilot programs often fit within existing O&M budgets.

technologySystems.name: SCADA / AMI (Advanced Metering Infrastructure)
technologySystems.description: Real-time monitoring and control for water, energy, and transit. AMI adds consumption analytics, leak detection, and demand response.

technologySystems.name: GIS / Asset Management (EAM)
technologySystems.description: Spatial asset inventory, condition tracking, and capital planning. Enables linear asset management for pipes, roads, and transmission lines.

technologySystems.name: CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System)
technologySystems.description: Work order management, preventive maintenance scheduling, asset history, and labor/material tracking for infrastructure maintenance.

technologySystems.name: ITS / AVL (Intelligent Transportation Systems / Automatic Vehicle Location)
technologySystems.description: Real-time vehicle tracking, passenger information, signal priority, and service disruption management for transit and traffic.

technologySystems.name: AFC (Automated Fare Collection)
technologySystems.description: Tap/card/mobile payment, account-based ticketing, and revenue protection for transit systems.

technologySystems.name: ADMS / DMS (Advanced Distribution Management System)
technologySystems.description: Real-time distribution grid monitoring, outage management, switching, and DER integration for electric utilities.

technologySystems.name: OMS (Outage Management System)
technologySystems.description: Customer outage reporting, crew dispatch, restoration tracking, and predictive outage analytics.

technologySystems.name: Pavement / Bridge Management System
technologySystems.description: Condition assessment data, deterioration modeling, treatment selection optimization, and capital program prioritization.

technologySystems.name: Airport Operations Management System (AOMS)
technologySystems.description: FIDS, gate management, resource allocation, Part 139 inspection tracking, and airfield condition reporting.

technologySystems.name: FOD Detection System
technologySystems.description: Radar or camera-based foreign object debris detection on runways and taxiways.

technologySystems.name: Broadband Mapping / Availability System
technologySystems.description: Serviceable location database, coverage mapping, challenge processing, and BEAD compliance reporting.

technologySystems.name: NG911 ESInet / i3 Platform
technologySystems.description: IP-based emergency services network, GIS-driven call routing, multimedia handling, and PSAP interoperability.

regulatoryFactors.name: Safe Drinking Water Act (SDWA)
regulatoryFactors.regulation: 42 U.S.C. 300f; 40 CFR Parts 141-143; EPA National Primary Drinking Water Regulations
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Continuous; MCL compliance ongoing; LCRR deadline 2024; PFAS MCL effective 2024-2029
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: EPA enforcement actions; civil penalties up to $66,086/day; consent decrees; criminal liability for knowing violations

regulatoryFactors.name: EPA Consent Decrees (Water/Wastewater)
regulatoryFactors.regulation: Clean Water Act 33 U.S.C. 1251; SDWA 42 U.S.C. 300f; negotiated settlement with DOJ/EPA
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Systems with enforcement actions or self-reported violations
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Contempt of court; stipulated penalties ($5K-$100K+ per violation); injunctive relief expansion

regulatoryFactors.name: FTA Requirements (49 CFR)
regulatoryFactors.regulation: 49 CFR Parts 630-674; NTD reporting; ADA compliance; Title VI; DBE; drug/alcohol testing
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All recipients of FTA funds (6,800+ agencies)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: FTA withhold; repayment; suspension; debarment; civil rights compliance actions

regulatoryFactors.name: FAA Part 139 - Airport Certification
regulatoryFactors.regulation: 14 CFR Part 139; AC 150/5200 series; ARFF requirements; pavement standards
regulatoryFactors.applicability: 3,300+ certificated airports serving scheduled passenger operations
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Certificate action; operations specification amendment; civil penalty; emergency order

regulatoryFactors.name: FERC / NERC CIP
regulatoryFactors.regulation: 16 CFR Part 824; 18 CFR Parts 35-39; NERC CIP-002 through CIP-014; FERC Order 706
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Bulk electric system owners, operators, users; applicable to municipal utilities if interconnected
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Continuous; CIP standards version updates; compliance audits every 3 years
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: NERC penalties up to $1.5M per violation per day; FERC enforcement referral; reliability standards violation

regulatoryFactors.name: BEAD / NTIA Broadband Programs
regulatoryFactors.regulation: Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) Division F; NTIA BEAD, Digital Equity, Middle Mile; 47 U.S.C. 1301
regulatoryFactors.applicability: States, territories, tribal lands, broadband offices receiving BEAD allocations
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Initial proposals 2023-2024; subgrantee selection 2024-2025; build completion by 2029-2030
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Funding clawback; NTIA corrective action; delay of future allocations; compliance review

regulatoryFactors.name: Clean Water Act NPDES
regulatoryFactors.regulation: 33 U.S.C. 1251; 40 CFR Parts 122-136; NPDES permits; SSO rule; MS4 permits
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All wastewater dischargers; municipal separate storm sewer systems
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Permit renewal every 5 years; continuous compliance; SSO reporting within 24 hours
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: EPA/state enforcement; civil penalties up to $66,086/day; consent decrees; criminal for knowing endangerment

regulatoryFactors.name: NextGen 911 / FCC Regulations
regulatoryFactors.regulation: 47 CFR 20.18 (wireless 911); FCC Report and Order 15-9; NENA i3 standard; state 911 statutes
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All PSAPs; wireless carriers; VoIP providers; NG911 system operators
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Text-to-911 deployed; location accuracy deadlines; state NG911 plans ongoing
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: FCC enforcement; state funding withholding; liability for failed emergency response

regulatoryFactors.name: State RPS / ZEV Mandates
regulatoryFactors.regulation: State-specific renewable portfolio standards; zero-emission vehicle mandates; fleet electrification orders
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Municipal utilities (RPS); public fleets (ZEV); varies by state
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: State enforcement; loss of incentive eligibility; compliance payments; reputational

regulatoryFactors.name: IIJA / IRA Formula Fund Compliance
regulatoryFactors.regulation: Pub.L. 117-58 (IIJA); Pub.L. 117-169 (IRA); FHWA/FTA/EPA apportionment; Buy America; Davis-Bacon; equity screening
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All recipients of IIJA/IRA infrastructure formula and competitive grants
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Obligation deadlines (typically 3-5 years); project completion per award terms
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Lapse of funds; repayment; suspension from future awards; single audit findings

workedExamples.title: Water Utility: Pipe Replacement ROI from Break Reduction
workedExamples.scenario: Mid-sized water utility, 800 miles of distribution, break rate 32/100 miles/year, 60% cast iron >50 years old. Annual emergency repair cost $3.2M, water loss valued at $800K, emergency overtime $400K.
workedExamples.interventionDescription: Deploy risk-based pipe replacement program targeting 1.5% of system annually (12 miles) with GIS-driven prioritization and trenchless technology (CIPP, pipe bursting).
assumptions: Replacement reduces break rate by 40% on replaced segments
Program funded via SRF loan at 2% interest

calculatedImpact.metric: Break rate reduced to 18/100 miles by Year 5; 144 breaks/year
calculatedImpact.netAnnualValue: $800K by Year 5; cumulative NPV $6.2M over 10 years
calculatedImpact.confidence: HIGH (validated with AWWA break rate elasticity and trenchless cost data)

workedExamples.title: Transit Agency: On-Time Performance and Ridership Recovery
workedExamples.scenario: Urban transit agency, 12M annual ridership pre-pandemic, currently at 8M. OTP 73%, farebox recovery 22%, operator vacancy 18%. Customer complaints up 35% YoY.
workedExamples.interventionDescription: Implement ITS/AVL with real-time dispatch optimization, automated run-cutting, and mobile driver apps. Upgrade AFC to account-based ticketing with mobile tap. Deploy targeted operator recruitment and retention program.
assumptions: OTP improvement of 10 percentage points → 4% ridership elasticity → 320K incremental trips
Overtime reduction of 30% via optimized scheduling
Fare evasion reduced from 9% to 5% via AFC

calculatedImpact.annualSavings: $1.26M overtime reduction + $1.28M fare revenue recovery + $400K customer service cost reduction = $2.94M
calculatedImpact.confidence: MEDIUM (ridership elasticity varies; validated with peer NTD data)

workedExamples.title: Municipal Electric Utility: SAIDI/SAIFI Reduction via ADMS and Grid Hardening
workedExamples.scenario: Municipal utility, 45K customers, SAIDI 280 minutes, SAIFI 2.2. 60% overhead distribution, vegetation-related outages 38%. Storm hardening deferred due to budget constraints.

baselineState.metric: SAIDI 280 min; SAIFI 2.2; 5.7 major event days

workedExamples.interventionDescription: Deploy ADMS with outage prediction, automated switching, and voltage optimization. Target 15% worst-performing feeders for undergrounding and recloser installation. Implement LiDAR-based vegetation management.
assumptions: ADMS reduces SAIDI by 20% on targeted circuits
Feeder hardening reduces storm impact by 40%
IRA Grid Resilience Grant covers 50% of hardening

calculatedImpact.confidence: MEDIUM (storm hardening effectiveness varies by region; ADMS benefits well-documented by IEEE)

buyingTriggers.trigger: EPA enforcement action or consent decree signed
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 0-12 months post-enforcement; high urgency for compliance technology and capital planning

buyingTriggers.trigger: FAA Part 139 inspection with findings or certificate action
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 0-6 months; immediate need for compliance tracking and operational documentation

buyingTriggers.trigger: BEAD award notification and initial proposal deadline
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 0-18 months; planning, compliance, and subgrantee technology procurement window

buyingTriggers.trigger: Major service disruption (water main break, transit strike, grid failure, bridge closure)
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 0-3 months post-event; political and board pressure for preventive investment

buyingTriggers.trigger: Ransomware or OT cybersecurity incident in peer utility (CISA alert)
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 0-6 months; board-directed cybersecurity assessment and remediation

buyingTriggers.trigger: New state ZEV mandate or federal fleet electrification deadline
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 12-36 months pre-deadline; fleet and infrastructure planning procurement

buyingTriggers.trigger: PFAS MCL final rule publication or state MCL adoption
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 6-24 months pre-compliance date; treatment technology and compliance monitoring procurement

buyingTriggers.trigger: Rate case filing or affordability study requirement
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 6-18 months pre-filing; need for cost efficiency demonstration and rate design support

buyingTriggers.trigger: New FTA Administrator or FTA triennial review scheduled
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 6-12 months pre-review; compliance documentation and data system preparation

buyingTriggers.trigger: NERC CIP audit notice or violation self-certification deadline
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 0-12 months; compliance management system and evidence collection

buyingTriggers.trigger: NextGen 911 state plan approval or PSAP consolidation mandate
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 6-24 months; ESInet procurement and PSAP technology upgrade

buyingTriggers.trigger: IIJA/IRA grant obligation deadline approaching (lapse risk)
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 6-18 months pre-obligation; project acceleration and technology procurement

buyingTriggers.trigger: Board or council directive for climate resilience / adaptation plan
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 6-18 months; infrastructure vulnerability assessment and hardening plan

buyingTriggers.trigger: Operator/driver/lineworker staffing crisis reaching service cut threshold
buyingTriggers.timingLogic: 0-6 months; workforce technology, automation, and retention program procurement

inheritedComponents: ValueDriverFramework (5 drivers: Revenue Uplift, Cost Savings, Risk Reduction, Mission Effectiveness, Working Capital/Cash Flow)
EvidenceSourceTypes (FASB/GASB, Federal Agencies, Industry Associations, etc.)
FormulaTemplates (Present Value, Risk-Adjusted, Investment Required, Payback Period, etc.)
SignalSourceTaxonomy (financial filings, audits, news, regulatory, credit, benchmarks, FOIA, signals)
BenchmarkMethodology (peer grouping, confidence scoring, confidence-adjusted benchmarks)
GovernanceFramework (evidence review, confidence scoring, customer-facing approval)

overriddenComponents.overrideReason: Subpack strictly extends master; no master components are overridden. All vertical-specialized content is additive.
